ocr.text: In the analysis of Emerson Electric's capital goods pricing trends for Q1 2024, the financial metrics reveal significant insights
into the company's performance within the industrial manufacturing and conglomerates sector. The Price-to-Earnings (P/E)
Ratio for Q1 2023 stood at 19.2, while it decreased to 18.5 in Q1 2024, reflecting a change of -0.7, which translates to a
The Current Ratio, which indicates the company's ability to cover short-term liabilities, improved from 1.80 in Q1 2023 to 1.75
in Q1 2024, marking a change of -0.05, or -2.78%. Additionally, the Debt-to-Equity ratio exhibited a slight decline from 0.47 in
Q1 2023 to 0.45 in Q1 2024, resulting in a change of -0.02, equivalent to a -4.26% shift. An analysis of the Gross Margin shows
an upward trend from 33% in Q1 2023 to 32% in Q1 2024, indicating a decrease of 1%, which reflects a -3.03% change year-
over-year. Furthermore, the Operating Margin also saw a decline from 16% in Q1 2023 to 15% in Q1 2024, resulting in a
change of -1%, which corresponds to a -6.25% year-over-year change. Collectively, these metrics highlight the financial
dynamics and shifts within Emerson Electric as it navigates the complexities of the capital goods market, providing a
comprehensive overview of its fiscal health and operational efficiency during this reporting period.
